attributes.name: macOS Credential Phishing via osascript Hidden Dialog
attributes.description: Detects osascript execution with display dialog and hidden answer parameters, the hallmark credential phishing technique used by AMOS, Banshee, and Cuckoo macOS stealers to create fake system password prompts. These stealers present convincing System Preferences dialogs to trick users into entering their macOS password.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name == "osascript" and process.args : "*display dialog*" and process.args : "*hidden answer*" and process.args : ("*password*", "*Password*", "*System Preferences*", "*macOS wants*", "*authenticate*", "*verify*", "*unlock*")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Legitimate AppleScript applications that prompt for passwords (rare with hidden answer flag)

attributes.name: macOS Credential Validation via dscl -authonly
attributes.description: Detects the dscl -authonly command used by all major macOS stealer families (AMOS, Banshee, Cuckoo) to validate phished user passwords before proceeding with keychain extraction. This is a critical link in the infostealer kill chain between credential phishing and credential dumping.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name == "dscl" and process.args : "*-authonly*" and not process.parent.executable : ("/System/Library/*", "/usr/libexec/*")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: System authentication processes (loginwindow, SecurityAgent) - excluded by parent process filter

attributes.name: macOS Keychain Credential Extraction via security Command
attributes.description: Detects the macOS security command being used to enumerate keychains, extract passwords, or dump keychain contents. AMOS and Banshee use security list-keychains and find-generic-password to extract credentials, particularly the Chrome Safe Storage key needed to decrypt browser credentials.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name == "security" and process.args : ("list-keychains", "find-generic-password", "dump-keychain", "find-internet-password", "export") and not process.parent.executable : ("/Applications/Google Chrome.app/*", "/Applications/Safari.app/*", "/Applications/Brave Browser.app/*", "/System/Library/*")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Browsers accessing their own keychain entries
IT management tools reading keychain metadata

attributes.name: macOS Multi-Browser Credential Database Access
attributes.description: Detects file access to browser credential databases (Login Data, Cookies, key4.db) across multiple browsers in rapid succession, a behavioral pattern unique to infostealer malware. AMOS targets 9+ browsers including Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Brave, Edge, Opera, Vivaldi, Chromium, and Arc.
attributes.query: sequence by process.entity_id with maxspan=5m [file where event.type == "access" and file.path : ("*Google/Chrome/Default/Login Data*", "*Google/Chrome/Default/Cookies*")] [file where event.type == "access" and file.path : ("*Firefox/Profiles*", "*Safari/Cookies/Cookies.binarycookies*", "*BraveSoftware/Brave-Browser*", "*Microsoft Edge/Default/Login Data*")]
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

attributes.name: macOS Cryptocurrency Wallet Extension Data Access
attributes.description: Detects access to cryptocurrency wallet browser extension storage directories. AMOS and Banshee target MetaMask, Coinbase, Phantom, Trust Wallet, Exodus, Atomic, Electrum, and Bitwarden extension data to steal private keys and wallet recovery phrases.
attributes.query: file where event.type == "access" and file.path : ("*nkbihfbeogaeaoehlefnkodbefgpgknn*", "*hnfanknocfeofbddgcijnmhnfnkdnaad*", "*bfnaelmomeimhlpmgjnjophhpkkoljpa*", "*egjidjbpglichdcondbcbdnbeeppgdph*", "*Exodus/exodus.wallet*", "*atomic/Local Storage/leveldb*", "*.electrum/wallets*", "*Bitwarden/data.json*") and not process.executable : ("/Applications/Google Chrome.app/*", "/Applications/Brave Browser.app/*", "/Applications/Exodus.app/*")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Browser processes accessing their own extension data
Cryptocurrency wallet applications accessing their own storage

attributes.name: macOS TCC Database Reset via tccutil
attributes.description: Detects execution of tccutil reset commands used by the Cuckoo Stealer to clear Transparency, Consent, and Control (TCC) database entries, forcing re-prompting of Automation permissions. This allows stealers to re-request AppleEvents access that was previously denied.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name == "tccutil" and process.args : "reset"
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: IT administrators resetting TCC permissions during troubleshooting

attributes.name: macOS Exfiltration Archive Creation (out.zip Pattern)
attributes.description: Detects creation of ZIP archive files commonly used by macOS infostealers (AMOS uses out.zip) for staging stolen data before HTTP POST exfiltration. Triggers on archive creation in temporary or user directories by non-standard processes.
attributes.query: file where event.type == "creation" and file.name : ("out.zip", "output.zip", "data.zip", "loot.zip", "steal.zip", "dump.zip") and not process.executable : ("/usr/bin/zip", "/usr/bin/ditto", "/System/Library/CoreServices/Finder.app/*", "/System/Library/CoreServices/Archive Utility.app/*")

technique.name: Archive Collected Data: Archive via Utility

author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Legitimate backup or compression tools creating similarly-named archives

attributes.name: macOS Infostealer Full Kill Chain Correlation
attributes.description: Correlates multiple macOS infostealer indicators within a 30-minute window: osascript credential phishing followed by keychain access and browser credential file access. This sequence is the complete AMOS/Banshee kill chain and represents a confirmed infostealer compromise.
attributes.query: sequence by host.id with maxspan=30m [process where event.type == "start" and process.name == "osascript" and process.args : "*hidden answer*"] [process where event.type == "start" and ((process.name == "dscl" and process.args : "*-authonly*") or (process.name == "security" and process.args : ("list-keychains", "find-generic-password")))] [file where event.type == "access" and file.path : ("*Login Data*", "*Cookies.binarycookies*", "*key4.db*")]
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Extremely unlikely - this multi-stage correlation has near-zero false positive rate

attributes.name: macOS Apple Notes Database Access by Non-Apple Process
attributes.description: Detects access to Apple Notes NoteStore.sqlite database by processes other than Notes.app or Spotlight indexing. AMOS stealer targets Apple Notes for harvesting passwords and sensitive data users store in notes, which is often overlooked by traditional credential monitoring.
attributes.query: file where event.type == "access" and (file.name == "NoteStore.sqlite" or file.path : "*group.com.apple.notes*") and not process.executable : ("/Applications/Notes.app/*", "/System/Library/Frameworks/*", "/usr/libexec/mds*", "/usr/libexec/mdworker*")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

attributes.name: macOS Cuckoo Stealer Hidden Credential Cache Directory
attributes.description: Detects creation of hidden directories matching the .local-UUID pattern with pw.dat credential cache files, a signature behavior of the Cuckoo Stealer variant. This directory is used to store phished passwords before keychain extraction.
attributes.query: file where event.type == "creation" and file.name == "pw.dat" and file.path regex~ """.*/\.local-[0-9a-f-]+/pw\.dat"""
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: None expected - .local-UUID/pw.dat is a highly specific Cuckoo Stealer pattern
